title: Fantasy Baseball Streamers and Roster Moves: Leiter, Bradley & More Pitching Updates on Tap.
body:
As the fantasy baseball season heats up, pitchers across the league are making adjustments to their arsenals, impacting their potential for success. This week, we're focusing on a trio of young starters who are making notable changes to their pitch mixes: Jack Leiter, Taj Bradley, and more. For fantasy owners looking to stream starts, these pitchers are now more intriguing than ever.

Jack Leiter, a 22-year-old righty pitcher for the Texas Rangers, has been a topic of interest in fantasy circles. His recent switch to a more fastball-heavy approach has raised eyebrows, as he's now throwing over 70% fastballs in his last three starts. This shift has led to a noticeable increase in strikeout rate, making him a more formidable force on the mound. With Leiter's ability to miss bats, he's become a streaming option to consider for owners looking for a quick boost.

Taj Bradley, a 22-year-old righty pitcher for the Tampa Bay Rays, has also been making waves with a change to his pitch mix. After relying heavily on his slider in the past, Bradley has begun to rely more on his four-seam fastball, which has seen a significant boost in velocity. This increased fastball velocity has allowed Bradley to generate more swing-and-miss opportunities, making him a more intriguing streaming option. However, owners should be aware that Bradley's control issues remain a concern, and he's still a riskier play than some other streaming options.

In addition to Leiter and Bradley, several other pitchers are making notable changes to their arsenals. For example, the Chicago Cubs' Cade Horton has increased his usage of his changeup, which has resulted in a lower ERA and more strikeouts. Meanwhile, the St. Louis Cardinals' Matthew Liberatore has seen a boost in velocity on his four-seam fastball, which has led to more swing-and-miss opportunities.

For fantasy owners, these changes in pitch mix offer both opportunities and challenges. On one hand, they create more streaming options for owners looking to capitalize on a hot streak. On the other hand, they can also make pitchers more unpredictable, making it harder to anticipate their performance. As with any fantasy decision, it's essential to stay up-to-date on the latest news and trends to make informed decisions.

As the season progresses, we can expect to see more pitchers making adjustments to their arsenals. It's essential for fantasy owners to stay flexible and adapt to these changes to maximize their success. With the right information and a keen eye for trends, owners can identify the streaming opportunities that will propel their teams to victory.
